summary: theme-color-picker ships install-time Windows DLL dropper with scheduled-task persistence
details: On `npm install`, the package's lifecycle hook executes `lib/chart-loader.js`, which downloads `payload.bin.enc` from `https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc`, XOR-decrypts the body with single-byte key 0x42, base64-decodes the result, validates an `MZ` PE header, writes the resulting DLL to `%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\<random>.dll`, and executes it via `rundll32.exe`. A scheduled task `WindowsUpdateService` is registered with `schtasks /create` (onlogon trigger, HIGHEST run level) for persistence, masquerading as a Windows Update service. After execution, the loader self-destructs: it spawns a `node -e` child that recursively `fs.rmSync`'s the module directory and rewrites the parent `package.json` to remove the dependency entry, erasing forensic traces. The package's `name` is `theme-color-picker` but its `description` claims to be a chart-analysis library and the loader references `analysis-chart` paths — the published identity does not correspond to any real functionality; the package exists solely to deliver the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
